author: codex-root

patch.value:
## User story

As a coding or planning agent, I want one bounded and explainable relationship query for an item so that I can understand its purpose, execution order, downstream impact, and supporting evidence without guessing or loading unrelated history.

## Token contract

Return the smallest useful summary first: item identity, state, immediate typed edges, critical path or blockers, evidence pointers, omitted counts, and a continuation token. Expand only on explicit demand or relevance thresholds.

## Explainability contract

Every included node and edge carries a concise selection reason, source, age or freshness, and whether it is authoritative, inferred, approximate, or user-authored.
As an agent, I can traverse why an item exists, what it affects, what blocks it, and its evidence chain from one bounded graph query
Provide one explainable, token-budgeted graph read that answers origin, prerequisites, dependents, impact, and evidence without separate full-tracker scans.

One request returns bounded ancestors, prerequisites, dependents, provenance, linked evidence, and the reason each node or edge was selected; Callers control direction, kind families, depth, node and edge limits, history window, token budget, fields, and cursor continuation; Default output is compact and counts-first; expanded and JSON or JSONL modes remain composable with grep, jq, and scripts; The response distinguishes complete, truncated, sampled, approximate, stale-index, and permission-redacted results; Public SDK, thin CLI, MCP action, and shell pipelines share one contract and deterministic ordering; Tests cover disconnected items, cycles in associative edges, missing endpoints, deep paths, concurrent mutations, and million-item bounded behavior
Make relationship context directly usable by coding agents through one predictable SDK and CLI flow
Agents spend fewer tokens and tool calls reconstructing lineage and can justify every surfaced relationship
Reduces missed prerequisites, stale assumptions, duplicated work, and unbounded context dumps
A bounded graph-context response with paths, semantic edge labels, evidence, truncation metadata, and continuation
Current context comes from several list, get, deps, comments, history, and search calls while the dependency graph is under-specified

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-13: all-status review found context packing and deps visualization items but no single agent story joining bounded lineage,impact,blockers,and evidence traversal; it belongs under pm-96h7.

message: Create bounded agent relationship context story

author: codex-root
message: Validation correction: retain the typed blocked_by dependency on pm-4jqm but clear the legacy scalar blocker field so an open planned story is not reported as stale-blocked

author: codex-root
message: Validation correction: restore the typed dependency after clearing the legacy scalar blocker field

author: codex-gpt5
message: Start broad SDK relationship events, analytics, bounded graph-context, and conformance implementation bundle

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation start 2026-07-14: build one bounded graph-context SDK response joining lineage, prerequisites, dependents, impact, evidence reasons, cost/truncation metadata, and continuation on the shared graph kernel.

value.note: bounded explainable graph-context packet with continuation

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: A graph-context packet stays agent-efficient when semantic reasons, evidence pointers, exact cost, omitted counts, and continuation share one bounded envelope instead of requiring separate deps, get, history, and files calls.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation evidence: added buildRelationshipContext for one-call bounded graph context with semantic reasons, root details/evidence, node/edge/depth/token budgets, exact inspected/omitted cost metadata, cancellation, and query-fingerprinted continuation cursors. Fresh installed-package proof returned design/release context around build through the public SDK. Remaining CLI/MCP adapters stay explicitly in this open story.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Remaining canonical scope after this PR: thin CLI, shell, and MCP parity plus million-item latency acceptance; keep story open.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #556 review round 1 evidence: bounded context now uses browser-safe TextEncoder byte sizing, charges root evidence exactly once, and explains custom hierarchy using registered endpoint orientation rather than labels. Direct ancestor/descendant and exact evidence token-cost regressions pass.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #556 review round 2 evidence: direct semantic reasons consume one indexed root incident snapshot, TextEncoder is reused, promoted evidence is emitted once outside root and charged exactly once, and oversized root/evidence baselines fail closed before returning under-budget metadata.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Pause after PR #556 SDK bounded-context tranche; remaining CLI/MCP/shell parity and million-item latency acceptance stay open.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Address PR #556 final review compatibility-documentation feedback

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #556 review round 3 evidence: documented deliberate precedence and hierarchy-direction fallbacks at bounded-context call sites for compatibility with legacy and JSON-parsed definitions; focused SDK tests, lint, duplicate detection, CodeFactor rules, and typecheck pass.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Review fix complete; remaining CLI and MCP scope stays open
